title: Lithocarpus dodonaeifolius
taxonomy: Lithocarpus dodonaeifolius, a species of plant, is classified as a member of the family Fagaceae. This family is commonly known as the beech family and includes species such as oak and beech trees. Within the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus dodonaeifolius is part of the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of evergreen trees native to Southeast Asia. The species is further classified as a species of evergreen tree, characterized by its glossy leaves and small acorns. As a member of the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus dodonaeifolius shares similarities with other species in this family, including the presence of a woody stem and deciduous or evergreen leaves.
uses: Lithocarpus dodonaeifolius is a tree species native to Southeast Asia, and its medicinal uses have been documented in traditional medicine. The tree's bark and leaves are reported to be used in the treatment of various health conditions. In traditional medicine, the bark is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in managing pain and reducing swelling. Additionally, the leaves are said to be used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lithocarpus dodonaeifolius for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Lithocarpus dodonaeifolius, also known as the Bornean tanang, is a tree species native to Borneo. Its leaves and bark have been traditionally used in folk medicine in Southeast Asia for various purposes.
